source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. RSE; 3% regard with disgust and hatred: Many people abhor snakes. #4 AMM REIA 4. | abhor the idea of leaving 不想离去

headline.core_en: to hate something very strongly, often for moral reasons

familiar_bridge.summary: hate 是普通的“讨厌、恨”；abhor 更正式、更强烈，常带有道德上或本能上的排斥。

core.main: abhor 不是轻微不喜欢，而是强烈到“无法接受、极其反感”。它常用来评价行为、观念或做法，而不只是个人喜好。

usage_groups.group_title: 强烈排斥某种行为或观念
common_patterns: abhor the idea of doing sth

examples.sentence: Many people abhor cruelty to animals.
examples.translation: 许多人憎恶虐待动物的行为。
examples.teaching_note: abhor 后面常接一种行为或做法，语气比 dislike/hate 更正式、更强烈。

examples.sentence: She abhorred the idea of sacrificing fairness for efficiency.
examples.translation: 她极其反感为了效率而牺牲公平的想法。
examples.teaching_note: abhor the idea of doing sth 常用于批评某种原则上不能接受的做法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He hates dishonesty in public life.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hate 最普通；abhor 更正式，排斥感更强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic detested the film’s sentimental ending.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: detest 也表示强烈讨厌，语气比 hate 更重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She loathed the hypocrisy of the committee.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: loathe 和 abhor 都很强，loathe 更偏个人情绪，abhor 更常带道德判断。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reformers embraced the new policy.
antonyms.difference_note: embrace 表示主动接受，方向与 abhor 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The practice was abhorrent to many observers.
The policy was met with public abhorrence.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. "JA; 让人反感的 inspiring disgust and loathing; repugnant: Racial discrimination was abhorrent to us all. 我们都很反感种族歧视

headline.core_cn: 令人憎恶的；极其反感的
headline.core_en: causing strong disgust or moral dislike

familiar_bridge.summary: 在 abhorrent 这类表达里，abhorrent 的核心是“令人憎恶的”。

core.main: abhorrent 的核心是“令人憎恶的；极其反感的”，要结合它作用的对象和句中的判断来理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 令人憎恶的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: abhorrent 表示“令人憎恶的；极其反感的”。

examples.sentence: Racial discrimination was abhorrent to us all.
examples.translation: 这句原书/来源例句体现了“令人憎恶的”。
examples.teaching_note: abhorrent 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The critic described the work as abhorrent because it fit the situation so well.
examples.translation: 这里 abhorrent 表示“令人憎恶的；极其反感的”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The joke was offensive rather than funny.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: offensive 更常见，语气可轻可重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy seemed morally repugnant to many voters.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: repugnant 和 abhorrent 都强调强烈反感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new design was appealing to younger readers.
antonyms.difference_note: appealing 与 abhorrent 方向相反。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 删节; 缩短 shorten (something) by cutting off the top or the end: truncate a news item 删节一条新闻 it G44] shorten

headline.core_en: to shorten something by cutting off part of it

familiar_bridge.summary: shorten 是“变短”；truncate 更像“从末端或某一处截掉一部分”，常用于文字、数据、发言或过程。

core.main: truncate 强调把原本完整的东西截短，尤其是删掉后半部分或末端，因此会带来“不完整”的感觉。

usage_groups.group_title: 把文字、数据或过程截短

examples.sentence: The editor truncated the article to fit the limited space.
examples.translation: 编辑删节了这篇文章，以适应有限的版面。
examples.teaching_note: truncate a text/report/article 强调删短后内容不再完整。

examples.sentence: A truncated data set can make the trend look more dramatic than it really is.
examples.translation: 被截短的数据集可能让趋势看起来比实际更剧烈。
examples.teaching_note: GRE 阅读中常见于数据或证据被截取后造成误导。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher shortened the assignment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: shorten 更宽泛；truncate 强调“截掉一部分”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The publisher abridged the novel for younger readers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abridge 常用于书、文章等文本删节。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new rules curtailed public debate.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: curtail 常指限制活动、权利或过程的长度/范围。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee extended the deadline.
antonyms.difference_note: extend 与 truncate 的方向相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The graph used a truncated scale.
The truncation of the quotation changed its meaning.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 一丝不苟的; 循规蹈矩的 showing great attention to detail or correct behavior: 4 nurse should be punctilious in following the doctor's orders. 护士应该一丝不苟地执行医嘱

headline.core_cn: 一丝不苟的；循规蹈矩的
headline.core_en: to detail or correct behavior

familiar_bridge.summary: 在 punctilious 这类表达里，punctilious 的核心是“一丝不苟的”。

core.main: punctilious 的核心是“一丝不苟的；循规蹈矩的”，要结合它作用的对象和句中的判断来理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 一丝不苟的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: punctilious 表示“一丝不苟的；循规蹈矩的”。

examples.sentence: 4 nurse should be punctilious in following the doctor's orders.
examples.translation: 这句原书/来源例句体现了“一丝不苟的”。
examples.teaching_note: punctilious 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The critic described the work as punctilious because it fit the situation so well.
examples.translation: 这里 punctilious 表示“一丝不苟的；循规蹈矩的”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A careful reader notices the small contradiction.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: careful 更日常，先帮助理解“认真、不粗心”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The historian made a meticulous record of each source.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: meticulous 强调细节上非常认真。

antonyms.example_sentence: A careless summary can distort the argument.
antonyms.difference_note: careless 是直接反向。

extension.root_affix: -ous 是常见后缀，常提示“形容词后缀，表示具有某种性质”。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 区分; 区别; 鉴别 recognize or ascertain what makes (someone or something) different: differentiate varieties of plants 鉴别各种各样的植物 | differentiate man from beast 区别人兽

headline.core_en: to recognize or show the difference between things

familiar_bridge.summary: different 是“不同”；differentiate 就是把不同点看出来、说清楚，或让两个东西变得不同。

core.main: differentiate 的重点是“看出差别并区分开”。在学术阅读里，它常用于区分概念、群体、原因或证据。

usage_groups.group_title: 区分相近概念或对象
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 differentiate A from B, differentiate between A and B，强调不要混淆两个对象。

examples.sentence: The study differentiates correlation from causation.
examples.translation: 这项研究区分了相关性和因果关系。
examples.teaching_note: differentiate A from B 是学术阅读中很常见的结构。

examples.sentence: Botanists differentiate these plants by the shape of their leaves.
examples.translation: 植物学家根据叶片形状来鉴别这些植物。
examples.teaching_note: differentiate 也可以表示根据特征“鉴别”类别。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The author distinguishes myth from historical fact.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: distinguish 是最直接的近义词。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A careful reader can discern a shift in the author’s tone.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: discern 更强调看出细微、不明显的差别。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The test discriminates between superficial and deep understanding.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: discriminate 在中性语境可表示“辨别差异”，但也可表示歧视，要看语境。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay conflates popularity with artistic value.
antonyms.difference_note: conflate 是把不同东西混成一类，正好和 differentiate 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The two proposals are different in purpose.
The difference lies in the method, not the goal.
Product differentiation helps companies avoid direct competition.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 假说; 猜想 a supposition or proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence as a starting point for further investigation: Other chemists rejected his hypothesis. 其他的化学家反对这一假说 。 复数 hypotheses

headline.core_en: a proposed explanation that can be tested with evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: guess 是随口猜；hypothesis 是基于已有线索提出、等待证据检验的“假说”。

core.main: hypothesis 不是已经证明的事实，而是一个可被检验的解释。它常出现在科学研究、历史解释和学术论证中。

usage_groups.group_title: 提出并检验一个解释

examples.sentence: Other chemists rejected his hypothesis because the evidence was too weak.
examples.translation: 其他化学家反对他的假说，因为证据太薄弱。
examples.teaching_note: reject a hypothesis 表示研究者认为这个解释站不住脚。

examples.sentence: The new fossils support the hypothesis that the species migrated earlier than previously believed.
examples.translation: 新的化石支持这一假说：该物种迁徙的时间比过去认为的更早。
examples.teaching_note: hypothesis 常用于解释证据背后的原因或机制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her answer was only a guess.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: guess 更随意；hypothesis 更正式，并且应当能被证据检验。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument rests on a questionable assumption.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assumption 是论证中先接受的前提，不一定会被实验检验。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery challenged the prevailing theory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: theory 通常更成体系；hypothesis 更像待检验的具体解释。

antonyms.example_sentence: The measurement is a fact, but its cause remains a hypothesis.
antonyms.difference_note: fact 是已确认的信息；hypothesis 是待验证的解释。

word_family.example_sentence: The researchers compared several competing hypotheses.
The professor used a hypothetical case to explain the rule.
Scientists hypothesize that the change was caused by climate pressure.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 完美的; 无可挑剔的 (of behavior, performance, or appearance) in accordance with the highest standards of propriety; faultless: speak impeccable English 说一口流利的英语 | impeccable good manners 无可挑剔的端庄举止 i 44 flawless

headline.core_en: so good or careful that no fault can be found

familiar_bridge.summary: perfect 是普通的“完美”；impeccable 更像“挑不出毛病”，常用于举止、表现、品味或语言。

core.main: impeccable 强调标准很高、几乎没有瑕疵。它常带有赞赏意味，尤其用于 manners, taste, logic, performance, English 等。

usage_groups.group_title: 表现、举止或品味无可挑剔

examples.sentence: She spoke impeccable English throughout the interview.
examples.translation: 她在整个面试中说着无可挑剔的英语。
examples.teaching_note: impeccable English 强调语言准确、自然，几乎没有错误。

examples.sentence: The critic praised the dancer’s impeccable timing.
examples.translation: 评论家称赞这位舞者无可挑剔的节奏把握。
examples.teaching_note: impeccable 常用于评价专业表现。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan looked perfect on paper.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: perfect 更普通；impeccable 更强调“挑不出错”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her performance was nearly flawless.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: flawless 侧重没有缺陷；impeccable 还常带有礼仪、品味或专业水准。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report was detailed and faultless.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: faultless 和 impeccable 接近，但语气更直白。

antonyms.example_sentence: The analysis was flawed from the start.
antonyms.difference_note: flawed 与 impeccable 形成直接对照。

extension.root_affix: im- 在这里表示否定；pecc- 和 fault / sin 的观念有关，所以 impeccable 可理解为“没有可指摘之处”。

word_family.example_sentence: The essay was impeccably organized.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. #2i8# HI; 完美的 showing a high degree of skill and flair; complete or perfect: a consummate master of violin 小提琴大师 | a consummate liar 撒谎大王

headline.core_cn: 技艺高超的；完全的；极其典型的
headline.core_en: showing complete skill or being a perfect example of a type

familiar_bridge.summary: complete 是“完整”；consummate 形容人或表现时，强调技艺已经到很成熟、很高超的程度。

core.main: consummate 作形容词时常表示“高超到几乎完美”。它也可表示某人是某类人的典型代表，比如 a consummate liar。

usage_groups.group_title: 技艺或表现非常高超
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 技艺高超的；炉火纯青的

examples.sentence: She was a consummate professional, calm even under intense pressure.
examples.translation: 她是一位极其专业的人，即使在巨大压力下也很冷静。
examples.teaching_note: a consummate professional 表示专业能力和表现都非常成熟。

examples.sentence: The violinist was a consummate master of tone and phrasing.
examples.translation: 这位小提琴家在音色和乐句处理上堪称大师。
examples.teaching_note: 原书中的 master 语境体现了 consummate 的“技艺高超”。

usage_groups.group_title: 某类人的典型代表

examples.sentence: He was a consummate liar, able to sound sincere even when inventing every detail.
examples.translation: 他是个十足的骗子，即使每个细节都是编的，也能听起来很真诚。
examples.teaching_note: 这里不是“完美”褒义，而是“某种特质非常典型、非常彻底”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The skillful negotiator kept both sides at the table.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: skillful 更普通；consummate 强调技艺达到很高水平。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 技艺娴熟的；掌控力强的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conductor gave a masterful performance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: masterful 和 consummate 都可赞扬高超表现。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She is an accomplished pianist.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accomplished 强调已经取得高水平成就。

antonyms.example_sentence: The presentation looked amateurish despite its ambitious goal.
antonyms.difference_note: amateurish 与 consummate 的成熟高超相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The argument was consummately structured.
The treaty marked the consummation of years of negotiation.

related_words.example_sentence: Anxiety consumed his attention.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. %#98; @t rebuff, ignore, or spurn disdainfully: She snubbed her neighbors by silence. 她对 REFBTR, ARE.

headline.core_en: to deliberately ignore or reject someone in a rude way

familiar_bridge.summary: ignore 是“不理”；snub 多了一层社交上的冷淡和轻视，像故意不给面子。

core.main: snub 的重点不是单纯没注意到，而是有意冷落、拒绝或怠慢别人，因此会让对方感到被轻视。

examples.sentence: The artist interpreted the museum’s silence as a public snub.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee rebuffed the proposal without discussion.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The omission seemed to slight the younger scholars.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: slight 更强调轻视或不给应有尊重。

antonyms.example_sentence: The community welcomed the new residents.
antonyms.difference_note: welcome 与 snub 的冷落相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The snubbed guest left early.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 恶化; 退化 the process of becoming decadent; the quality or state of being decadent: escape the decadence that attends upon old age & # = #% #7 KH KH #5044] degeneracy 9 decadent /'dekadant/ adj. 退化的; 恶化的; 衰退的 marked by decay or decline: a decadent phase 堕落阶段

headline.core_en: moral, cultural, or social decline after a period of strength

familiar_bridge.summary: decline 是普通的“衰退”；decadence 更常指文化、社会或道德状态从繁盛走向腐化、衰败。

core.main: decadence 不只是变差，而是带有“曾经有活力或标准，现在变得奢靡、腐化、失去生命力”的意味。

usage_groups.group_title: 文化或道德走向衰败

examples.sentence: The historian linked the empire’s decadence to its growing indifference to public duty.
examples.translation: 这位历史学家把帝国的衰败和它日益漠视公共责任联系起来。
examples.teaching_note: decadence 常用于历史或文化评论，强调内部活力和道德标准的下降。

examples.sentence: The novel portrays luxury not as refinement but as a sign of decadence.
examples.translation: 这部小说把奢华描写成衰败的迹象，而不是高雅的表现。
examples.teaching_note: decadence 常和 excessive luxury 或 moral decline 联系在一起。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The city’s decline began after the factories closed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: decline 更宽泛；decadence 更常带道德或文化腐化感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic saw the trend as a degeneration of public taste.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: degeneration 强调从较好状态退化到较差状态。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The investigation exposed corruption within the agency.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: corruption 更偏腐败或道德败坏；decadence 更偏整体文化/社会衰败。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reforms brought a period of cultural renewal.
antonyms.difference_note: renewal 表示重新获得活力，和 decadence 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The critic called the court’s extravagant rituals decadent.
The abandoned buildings showed signs of decay.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. i confuse: conflate past and present 混淆过去和现在

headline.core_cn: 混淆；把不同事物合并看待
headline.core_en: to combine two different ideas or things as if they were the same

familiar_bridge.summary: confuse 是普通的“弄混”；conflate 常指在论证中把两个本来不同的概念合成一个来谈。

core.main: conflate 的重点是“把不同东西混成一类”，于是差别被抹掉，论证也可能变得不准确。

usage_groups.group_title: 把两个概念混为一谈

examples.sentence: The essay conflates popularity with artistic value.
examples.translation: 这篇文章混淆了受欢迎程度和艺术价值。
examples.teaching_note: conflate A with B 表示把两个不同标准当成同一回事。

examples.sentence: It is misleading to conflate past and present when judging the policy.
examples.translation: 评价这项政策时把过去和现在混为一谈是有误导性的。
examples.teaching_note: 原书的 past and present 语境体现了 conflate 的核心。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report confused cause with effect.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confuse 更普通；conflate 更常用于概念或论证。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor merged the two sections into one chapter.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: merge 可以是中性合并；conflate 往往暗示不该合并。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The film blurs the line between memory and fantasy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: blur 强调边界变模糊，和 conflate 的效果接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author differentiates evidence from speculation.
antonyms.difference_note: differentiate 是把差别说清楚，方向与 conflate 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The conflation of wealth with virtue weakens the argument.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 粗略的; 不详尽的 not thorough or detailed: be sketchy about some important details 对一些重要细节粗粗涉及 | a sketchy knowledge of geography 粗浅的地理知识 #5044 superficial

headline.core_en: not thorough or detailed

familiar_bridge.summary: 在 sketchy 这类表达里，sketchy 的核心是“粗略的”。

core.main: sketchy 的核心是“粗略的；不详尽的”，要结合它作用的对象和句中的判断来理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 粗略的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: sketchy 表示“粗略的；不详尽的”。

examples.sentence: be sketchy about some important details.
examples.translation: 这句原书/来源例句体现了“粗略的”。
examples.teaching_note: sketchy 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The critic described the work as sketchy because it fit the situation so well.
examples.translation: 这里 sketchy 表示“粗略的；不详尽的”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The description seemed superficial once the evidence was considered.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: superficial 和 sketchy 接近，但语气、对象或正式程度可能不同。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The report offered only a sketch of the plan.
confusable_words.difference_note: sketch 和 sketchy 属于同一词族；重点区别词性、搭配和句中位置。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The proposal was sketchily explained.
confusable_words.difference_note: sketchily 和 sketchy 属于同一词族；重点区别词性、搭配和句中位置。

word_family.example_sentence: The report offered only a sketch of the plan.
The proposal was sketchily explained.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 遗责; 公开指责 publicly declare to be wrong or evil: denounce sb. for neglect of duty 指责某 AGE | denounce an aggressive war ii FAZER #044 lambaste #4 denunciation /dinansrerfan/ n. 遗责;公开指责 public condemnation of someone or something: a denunciation of violence i# %#&H 3 5444 indictment

headline.core_en: to publicly criticize someone or something as wrong or evil

familiar_bridge.summary: criticize 是普通的“批评”；denounce 是公开、强烈地谴责，常带道德判断。

core.main: denounce 强调在公开场合明确指出某事错误、邪恶或不可接受。它比 criticize 更强，常用于政治、战争、暴力、腐败等议题。

usage_groups.group_title: 公开谴责错误或不义行为
common_patterns: denounce someone for neglect of duty

examples.sentence: The senator denounced the use of violence against civilians.
examples.translation: 这位参议员公开谴责对平民使用暴力。
examples.teaching_note: denounce 后面常接被认为严重错误的行为或政策。

examples.sentence: The report denounced officials for neglect of duty.
examples.translation: 这份报告指责官员玩忽职守。
examples.teaching_note: denounce someone for sth. 表示因某事公开谴责某人。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The reviewer criticized the argument for lacking evidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 语气可轻可重；denounce 更公开、更强烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editorial condemned the policy as unjust.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: condemn 与 denounce 接近，都带强烈否定判断。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The columnist lambasted the mayor for ignoring the crisis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lambaste 更口语化一些，强调猛烈抨击。

antonyms.example_sentence: Several scientists endorsed the new guidelines.
antonyms.difference_note: endorse 表示支持，方向与 denounce 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The speech was a denunciation of political violence.
The denounced policy was withdrawn within a week.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 能渗透的; 透 ( 气 、 光等 ) 的 having many small holes that allow water or air to pass through slowly: The clay pot is porous. 这个土绒渗水 。 #444 permeable

headline.core_cn: 多孔可渗透的；防线不严密的
headline.core_en: having small holes that let liquid, air, or ideas pass through

familiar_bridge.summary: pore 是“孔”；porous 就是有很多小孔，所以水、空气或信息能穿过去。

core.main: porous 先是物理上的“多孔、可渗透”，也可比喻边界、制度或防线不严，容易被穿过。

usage_groups.group_title: 材料能让水或空气通过

examples.sentence: The clay pot is porous, so water slowly seeps through it.
examples.translation: 这个陶罐是多孔的，所以水会慢慢渗出来。
examples.teaching_note: porous 在材料语境中强调有孔、能渗透。

usage_groups.group_title: 边界或制度容易被穿透
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不严密的；容易被突破的

examples.sentence: The country’s porous borders made illegal trade difficult to control.
examples.translation: 该国边境防线不严，使非法贸易难以控制。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是比喻用法：边界像多孔材料一样容易被穿过。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The membrane is permeable to water but not to larger molecules.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: permeable 更技术化；porous 更强调材料有孔。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The defense line proved penetrable under pressure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: penetrable 常用于屏障或防御可被穿透。

antonyms.example_sentence: The coating made the fabric impermeable to rain.
antonyms.difference_note: impermeable 是 porous/permeable 的直接反义。

word_family.example_sentence: Water entered the rock through tiny pores.
High porosity allows the soil to hold more water.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 极为热衷的; 酷爱的 having or showing a keen interest in or enthusiasm for something: an avid reader of science fiction 科幻小说迷派生 avidity /a'vidoti/ n. 极度热衷; 极度热情 extreme eagerness or enthusiasm: He read detective stories with avidity. (1/424 % BHIE HER ik.

headline.core_en: very eager or enthusiastic about something

familiar_bridge.summary: eager 是“急切的”；avid 更像长期、强烈地热衷于某事，常用来形容读者、粉丝或收藏者。

core.main: avid 强调兴趣强烈、投入度高，不是偶尔喜欢，而是很积极、很着迷。

usage_groups.group_title: 对某事非常热衷

examples.sentence: She is an avid reader of science fiction.
examples.translation: 她是科幻小说的狂热读者。
examples.teaching_note: an avid reader/fan/collector 是最自然的搭配。

examples.sentence: The historian had an avid interest in forgotten local traditions.
examples.translation: 这位历史学家对被遗忘的地方传统有浓厚兴趣。
examples.teaching_note: avid interest 强调兴趣强烈而持久。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The students were eager to begin the project.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: eager 更宽泛；avid 更强调长期热情或强烈兴趣。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The audience was enthusiastic about the new performance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: enthusiastic 侧重情绪热烈；avid 更常和兴趣对象搭配。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reformer was zealous in defending civil liberties.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: zealous 常带信念或使命感，比 avid 更正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: Many voters remained indifferent to the issue.
antonyms.difference_note: indifferent 与 avid 的热衷相反。

word_family.example_sentence: He read detective stories with avidity.
The children listened avidly to the story.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. iff; {#8 win or regain the favor of (a god, spirit, or person) by doing something that pleases them: propitiate the gods with sacrifices 用献祭取悦神明

headline.core_en: to please or calm someone powerful or angry in order to gain favor

familiar_bridge.summary: please 是“取悦”；propitiate 更像为了让有权力或正在生气的一方息怒而去讨好、安抚。

core.main: propitiate 常带宗教或权力关系色彩：一方通过献祭、让步或讨好来换取神明、统治者或公众的好感。

usage_groups.group_title: 通过讨好来安抚强势一方

examples.sentence: Ancient communities offered sacrifices to propitiate the gods.
examples.translation: 古代社群献祭以取悦神明、求其息怒。
examples.teaching_note: propitiate the gods 是典型宗教语境。

examples.sentence: The leader made concessions to propitiate angry critics.
examples.translation: 这位领导人作出让步，以安抚愤怒的批评者。
examples.teaching_note: 现代语境中也可指通过让步来平息不满。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The government lowered taxes to appease angry voters.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: appease 更常见，可带“姑息”意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The apology was meant to placate the offended community.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: placate 强调让生气的人平静下来。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The compromise mollified some opponents but not all.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mollify 更正式，强调缓和情绪或反对。

antonyms.example_sentence: The insult provoked an immediate response.
antonyms.difference_note: provoke 是激起怒气，和 propitiate 的安抚相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The ritual was an act of propitiation.
His propitiatory tone softened the dispute.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 7H#; 垂头丧气的; 情绪低落的 sad and depressed; dispirited: He stood in the street looking dejected. {= LAM 35 EB LE.

headline.core_en: sad and discouraged, especially after disappointment

familiar_bridge.summary: sad 是普通的“难过”；dejected 更像遭遇挫折后低头丧气、提不起精神。

core.main: dejected 强调失望后的低落状态，常通过人的表情、姿态或语气表现出来。

usage_groups.group_title: 失败或失望后情绪低落

examples.sentence: He stood in the street looking dejected after the rejection.
examples.translation: 遭到拒绝后，他站在街上，显得垂头丧气。
examples.teaching_note: look dejected 很自然，强调外在表情和姿态。

examples.sentence: The team seemed dejected after losing the final match.
examples.translation: 输掉决赛后，这支队伍显得很沮丧。
examples.teaching_note: dejected 常用于失败之后的情绪反应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She felt sad when the trip was canceled.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sad 更普通；dejected 更强调受挫后的低落。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His downcast expression revealed his disappointment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: downcast 很接近 dejected，常强调神情低落。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 极度沮丧的；失去希望的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The repeated failures left him despondent.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: despondent 比 dejected 更重，带失去希望感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The students were elated by the unexpected victory.
antonyms.difference_note: elated 表示非常高兴，和 dejected 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: A sense of dejection spread through the room.
He nodded dejectedly and left.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 痛骂; %#%; 猛烈择击 insulting, abusive, or highly critical language: face the torrent of invective 面对一顿痛吕 | a speech filled with invective 3 Wa th 74 PE HIB

headline.core_en: abusive or bitterly critical language

familiar_bridge.summary: insult 是“侮辱”；invective 指一连串尖刻、攻击性的辱骂或抨击，常出现在演讲、文章、争论中。

core.main: invective 不是普通批评，而是带有攻击性、侮辱性的语言。它强调说话方式很猛烈、很伤人。

usage_groups.group_title: 攻击性很强的辱骂或抨击

examples.sentence: The candidate faced a torrent of invective after the debate.
examples.translation: 辩论之后，这位候选人遭到一阵猛烈辱骂。
examples.teaching_note: torrent of invective 强调攻击性语言像洪流一样涌来。

examples.sentence: The article relied more on invective than on evidence.
examples.translation: 这篇文章更多依靠尖刻抨击，而不是证据。
examples.teaching_note: GRE 阅读中可用于批评论证情绪化、攻击性强。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The remark sounded like an insult rather than a joke.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: insult 可以是一次侮辱；invective 常指一段或一连串攻击性言辞。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The referee endured verbal abuse from the crowd.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abuse 可指辱骂，也可指虐待；invective 只指攻击性语言。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The review was full of vitriol.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vitriol 更强调语言恶毒、酸刻。

antonyms.example_sentence: The teacher’s praise encouraged the class.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 是正向评价，和 invective 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The pamphlet argued invectively rather than calmly.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 彻底革新; 全面修改 a thorough examination of machinery or a system, with repairs or changes made if necessary: a system which is in need of radical overhaul 需要根本改革的体制 | a major overhaul of the foreign policy 外交政策的重大修正

headline.core_en: a thorough repair, revision, or reform of a system

familiar_bridge.summary: repair 是“修”；overhaul 是把系统、机器或政策从里到外全面检查并大改。

core.main: overhaul 强调彻底、系统性的修理或改革，不是小修小补。它可作名词，也可作动词。

usage_groups.group_title: 系统、机器或政策的全面改革

examples.sentence: The education system is in need of a radical overhaul.
examples.translation: 教育体系需要一次根本性的改革。
examples.teaching_note: in need of an overhaul 表示现有系统问题较深，需要全面改。

examples.sentence: The company overhauled its hiring process after repeated complaints.
examples.translation: 在多次投诉之后，公司全面改革了招聘流程。
examples.teaching_note: overhaul 作动词时，强调对流程或制度进行系统性调整。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law reformed the old tax system.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reform 强调改进制度；overhaul 更强调彻底、全面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum revamped its exhibition space.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: revamp 常用于让东西焕然一新，语气比 overhaul 稍轻。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company restructured its departments to reduce costs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: restructure 侧重结构重组；overhaul 范围更宽。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee voted to preserve the existing rules.
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 表示保持原样，和 overhaul 的全面改动相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The overhauled system processed applications faster.
Overhauling the policy took several years.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 恢复的; 康复的 having the ability to restore health, strength, or a feeling of well-being: a restorative he 六帮助康复的草药 3 044 tonic

headline.core_en: helping to restore health, strength, or well-being

familiar_bridge.summary: restore 是“恢复”；restorative 形容能让体力、健康或精神状态恢复的东西。

core.main: restorative 强调“让人恢复过来”：恢复精力、健康、平静或幸福感。它常用于 sleep, rest, treatment, herb, effect。

usage_groups.group_title: 帮助身体或精神恢复

examples.sentence: A quiet walk had a restorative effect after a stressful week.
examples.translation: 紧张的一周之后，一次安静的散步起到了恢复身心的作用。
examples.teaching_note: restorative effect 表示某事让人重新恢复状态。

examples.sentence: The doctor recommended restorative sleep rather than another stimulant.
examples.translation: 医生建议通过恢复性的睡眠来调整，而不是再用兴奋剂。
examples.teaching_note: restorative sleep/rest 是很自然的搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The treatment had a healing effect on the wound.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: healing 更直接与伤口或疾病恢复有关。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The patient needed a long recuperative period.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: recuperative 更医学化，强调病后恢复。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The mountain air felt like a tonic after months in the city.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tonic 可作名词或形容词，强调提振精神或身体。

antonyms.example_sentence: The illness was debilitating for several months.
antonyms.difference_note: debilitating 会削弱体力，和 restorative 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The project restored the old theater.
The restoration of the painting took two years.
The retreat was designed to work restoratively.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 强烈反对; 对 … 强烈不满 feel or express strong disapproval of (something): deplore the use of nuclear weapons 强烈反对使用核武器派生 deplorable /drploxrsbl adj. 极为恶劣的; 值得遗责的 deserving strong condemnation: children living in deplorable conditions 生活在极为恶劣环境中的儿童近义词 reprehensible

headline.core_en: to strongly disapprove of or deeply regret something

familiar_bridge.summary: disapprove 是“不赞成”；deplore 更正式、更强烈，常表示对某种行为、状况或后果深感不满。

core.main: deplore 可以是公开表达强烈反对，也可以是对糟糕状况表示深切遗憾。对象通常是 violence, conditions, policy, use of weapons 等。

usage_groups.group_title: 强烈反对某种行为或状况
common_patterns: deplore the use of nuclear weapons

examples.sentence: The group deplored the use of nuclear weapons.
examples.translation: 该组织强烈反对使用核武器。
examples.teaching_note: deplore the use of... 是原书语境的核心结构。

examples.sentence: The report deplored the conditions in which many children were living.
examples.translation: 报告对许多儿童的恶劣生活条件表示强烈不满。
examples.teaching_note: deplore 也可用于对糟糕状况表示深切遗憾或谴责。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many residents disapproved of the new plan.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disapprove 更普通；deplore 更正式、更强烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court condemned the act as unlawful.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: condemn 更偏公开谴责；deplore 可带遗憾和反对。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The historian lamented the loss of local traditions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lament 更强调悲伤和痛惜；deplore 更强调强烈不满。

antonyms.example_sentence: The board approved the revised proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: approve 是正向认可，和 deplore 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The children were living in deplorable conditions.
The records were deplorably incomplete.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Al; Asi encourage or assist (someone) to do something wrong, in particular, to commit a crime or other offense: abet sb. in a crime 唆使某人犯罪 #44 instigate

headline.core_en: to help or encourage someone to do something wrong or illegal

familiar_bridge.summary: help 是“帮助”；abet 是帮助别人做坏事，尤其是犯罪或不正当行为。

core.main: abet 的重点不是普通帮忙，而是协助、怂恿或促成错误行为。法律语境中常见 aid and abet。

usage_groups.group_title: 协助或怂恿违法行为

examples.sentence: The accountant was accused of helping to abet the fraud.
examples.translation: 这名会计被指控协助促成这起欺诈。
examples.teaching_note: abet 常和 crime/fraud/offense 一类负面对象搭配。

examples.sentence: The law punishes those who aid and abet a crime.
examples.translation: 法律会惩罚协助和教唆犯罪的人。
examples.teaching_note: aid and abet 是固定法律表达，表示协助并促成犯罪。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She helped her friend prepare the documents.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: help 是中性；abet 专指帮人做坏事。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader was accused of instigating the riot.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: instigate 强调挑起或煽动；abet 强调协助或促成。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new software facilitated communication.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: facilitate 通常中性；abet 带明显负面或违法色彩。

antonyms.example_sentence: The warning helped prevent further damage.
antonyms.difference_note: prevent 是阻止坏事发生，与 abet 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The charge included abetment of illegal activity.
The court treated him as an abettor rather than a bystander.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. Wess; 不坚固的; 微弱的 very weak or slight: His tenuous daydream snapped. 他那胞弱的白日梦破灭了 。 | He preserved a tenuous contact with them. 他和他们保持着若即若离的关系 。 adj. 不可靠的; 站不住脚的 lacking stability; precarious: tenuous reasons 站不住脚的理由

headline.core_en: weak, slight, or not strongly supported

familiar_bridge.summary: weak 是普通的“弱”；tenuous 常形容联系、证据、理由或关系很薄弱，经不起推敲。

core.main: tenuous 的核心是“支撑不牢”。它可以说联系很弱、证据不足，也可以说处境或关系很脆弱。

usage_groups.group_title: 联系、证据或理由很薄弱

examples.sentence: The evidence for the theory remains tenuous.
examples.translation: 支持这一理论的证据仍然很薄弱。
examples.teaching_note: tenuous evidence 表示证据支撑不够强。

examples.sentence: The essay makes only a tenuous connection between diet and creativity.
examples.translation: 这篇文章只是在饮食和创造力之间建立了很牵强的联系。
examples.teaching_note: tenuous connection 强调联系不稳、不充分。

usage_groups.group_title: 关系或状态脆弱、不稳定

examples.sentence: He maintained only tenuous contact with his former colleagues.
examples.translation: 他和以前的同事只保持着很微弱的联系。
examples.teaching_note: 原书中 contact 的语境体现了“联系微弱、若即若离”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument is weak without evidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: weak 最普通；tenuous 更常用于抽象联系或理由。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agreement remained fragile after the dispute.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fragile 强调容易被破坏；tenuous 强调支撑薄弱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Their financial position was precarious.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: precarious 更强调处境不稳、有风险。

antonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion rests on solid evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: solid evidence/reasoning 与 tenuous 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The claims were tenuously linked to the data.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 紧迫的; 迫切的 pressing; demanding: exigent demands 人迫切的需求近义词 pressing

headline.core_en: urgent and demanding immediate attention

familiar_bridge.summary: urgent 是“紧急”；exigent 更正式，常说情况、需求或问题很急迫，要求马上处理。

core.main: exigent 强调外部压力很强、时间紧、不能拖延。它常用于 demands, circumstances, needs。

usage_groups.group_title: 情况或需求非常紧迫

examples.sentence: The hospital had to respond quickly to exigent medical needs.
examples.translation: 医院必须迅速应对紧迫的医疗需求。
examples.teaching_note: exigent needs 表示需求迫切，要求马上行动。

examples.sentence: The court allowed the search because of exigent circumstances.
examples.translation: 由于情况紧急，法院允许了这次搜查。
examples.teaching_note: exigent circumstances 是法律和正式语境中的常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The repair became urgent after the storm.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: urgent 更日常；exigent 更正式、更强调压力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The city faced pressing housing needs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pressing 和 exigent 接近，常修饰问题或需求。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: It is imperative to protect the evidence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: imperative 强调必须做；exigent 强调形势紧迫。

antonyms.example_sentence: The clinic postponed nonurgent appointments.
antonyms.difference_note: nonurgent 与 exigent 的紧迫感相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The plan changed because of the exigencies of war.

headline.core_en: of (someone or something), typically in a formal statement

examples.teaching_note: censure 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The article discussed censure as part of a broader argument.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The reviewer criticized the argument for lacking evidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 更日常，语气可轻可重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editorial condemned the policy as unfair.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: condemn 比 criticize 更强烈。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report praised the team's restraint.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 与批评方向相反。

headline.core_cn: 严厉批评的；喜欢严厉批评他人的
headline.core_en: of others

familiar_bridge.summary: 在 censorious 这类表达里，censorious 的核心是“严厉批评的”。

core.main: censorious 的核心是“严厉批评的；喜欢严厉批评他人的”，要结合它作用的对象和句中的判断来理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 严厉批评的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: censorious 表示“严厉批评的；喜欢严厉批评他人的”。

examples.sentence: be censorious of someone.
examples.translation: 这句原书/来源例句体现了“严厉批评的”。
examples.teaching_note: censorious 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The critic described the work as censorious because it fit the situation so well.
examples.translation: 这里 censorious 表示“严厉批评的；喜欢严厉批评他人的”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The reviewer criticized the argument for lacking evidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 更日常，语气可轻可重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editorial condemned the policy as unfair.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: condemn 比 criticize 更强烈。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report praised the team's restraint.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 与批评方向相反。

extension.root_affix: -ous 是常见后缀，常提示“形容词后缀，表示具有某种性质”。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (针对言论 、 出版物等的 ) 审查; 删改; 查禁 the suppression or prohibition of any parts of books, films, news, etc. that are considered obscene, politically unacceptable, or a threat to security: The regulation imposes censorship on all media. 规定要求对所有媒体进行审查

headline.core_en: the suppression or control of speech, publications, films, or news

familiar_bridge.summary: censor 是“审查者/审查”；censorship 指对言论、出版物、电影、新闻等进行删改或禁止。

core.main: censorship 的重点是权力机构控制信息流通，判断哪些内容不能公开出现。

usage_groups.group_title: 控制媒体、出版物或言论

examples.sentence: The regulation imposes censorship on all media.
examples.translation: 这项规定要求对所有媒体进行审查。
examples.teaching_note: impose censorship on... 表示对某类媒体或言论施加审查。

examples.sentence: Writers often challenged censorship by using irony and allegory.
examples.translation: 作家常通过讽刺和寓言来挑战审查制度。
examples.teaching_note: censorship 常和 freedom of expression 构成对照。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The state tried to control public information.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: control 很宽泛；censorship 专指对信息和表达的控制。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The suppression of dissent weakened public debate.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: suppression 更宽泛，可用于言论、抗议、证据等被压制。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The prohibition of the film drew criticism.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prohibition 强调正式禁止；censorship 强调审查和删改。

antonyms.example_sentence: Free expression allows unpopular ideas to be debated openly.
antonyms.difference_note: free expression 是 censorship 的价值对立面。

word_family.example_sentence: The editor refused to censor the report.
The censored article left out several political details.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 高度赞扬; PEA; 颂词 a speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly: the encomium to the king 对于国王的赞领近义词 tribute

headline.core_en: a formal speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly

familiar_bridge.summary: praise 是普通的“赞扬”；encomium 是正式、隆重的赞美文字或讲话。

core.main: encomium 通常不是随口夸一句，而是一段正式的颂扬，常用于人物、君主、作家、作品或成就。

usage_groups.group_title: 正式而高度的赞扬

examples.sentence: The poet wrote an encomium to the king.
examples.translation: 诗人为国王写了一篇颂词。
examples.teaching_note: encomium to someone 是原书语境的自然结构。

examples.sentence: The review was less a critique than an encomium to the director’s genius.
examples.translation: 这篇评论与其说是批评，不如说是对导演才华的颂扬。
examples.teaching_note: encomium 可用于指出某段文字过于赞美。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher’s praise encouraged the class.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: praise 最普通；encomium 更正式、更成篇。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The concert was a tribute to the composer.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tribute 常带纪念或致敬意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech became a panegyric to national unity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: panegyric 比 encomium 更书面、更隆重。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay offers criticism rather than praise.
antonyms.difference_note: criticism 与 encomium 的赞颂方向相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The article’s encomiastic tone made it seem less objective.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. AS22492 6%); 不受控制的 uncontrolled; unconstrained: unbridled enthusiasm 奔放的热情

headline.core_en: not controlled or restrained

familiar_bridge.summary: bridle 是马笼头，用来控制马；unbridled 就像“没有缰绳”，情绪、欲望或力量不受控制。

core.main: unbridled 形容某种情绪或力量完全放开，没有限制。它可以是热情，也可以是野心、贪婪或权力。

usage_groups.group_title: 情绪、欲望或力量不受控制

examples.sentence: The announcement was met with unbridled enthusiasm.
examples.translation: 这个消息引发了毫不掩饰的热情。
examples.teaching_note: unbridled enthusiasm 是原书中的核心搭配，通常偏正面。

examples.sentence: The essay warns against unbridled ambition in political leaders.
examples.translation: 这篇文章警告人们警惕政治领导人的不受约束的野心。
examples.teaching_note: unbridled 也可用于负面力量，如 greed/ambition/power。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fire spread in an uncontrolled way.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: uncontrolled 更直白；unbridled 更形象、更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crowd showed unrestrained joy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unrestrained 和 unbridled 很接近，强调没有约束。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Unchecked power can threaten civil liberties.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unchecked 常强调没有外部限制或监督。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her response was calm and restrained.
antonyms.difference_note: restrained 与 unbridled 的“无约束”相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The rider used the bridle to guide the horse.
His anger was bridled by a sense of duty.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 报酬; 酬劳 money paid for work or a service: introduce remuneration for advisers 开始实行顾问酬谢制

headline.core_en: payment for work or services

familiar_bridge.summary: pay 是普通的“工资/付款”；remuneration 更正式，常用于合同、顾问、董事、专业服务的报酬。

core.main: remuneration 强调为劳动或服务支付的报酬，语气正式，常出现在商业、法律和政策语境中。

usage_groups.group_title: 为工作或服务支付报酬

examples.sentence: The company introduced remuneration for outside advisers.
examples.translation: 公司开始为外部顾问提供酬劳。
examples.teaching_note: remuneration for advisers 是原书语境的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The board reviewed the executive remuneration package.
examples.translation: 董事会审查了高管薪酬方案。
examples.teaching_note: remuneration package 是商业语境常见搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The workers demanded higher pay.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: pay 更日常；remuneration 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The contract includes compensation for travel expenses.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: compensation 可指薪酬或补偿，范围更宽。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The interns received a small stipend.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stipend 通常指固定津贴，金额可能不高。

antonyms.example_sentence: Volunteer work is usually done without pay.
antonyms.difference_note: volunteer work 通常不以 remuneration 为目的。

word_family.example_sentence: The organization remunerated the consultants for their work.
The position was demanding but remunerative.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ”虚假; 错误 the fact of being untrue, incorrect, or insincere: the falsity of one's smile = AX 容中的虚情假义

headline.core_en: the state of being untrue, incorrect, or insincere

familiar_bridge.summary: false 是“假的”；falsity 是名词，指某个说法、表情或印象的虚假性。

core.main: falsity 常用于分析话语、证据、微笑、论点是否真实。它可以指事实错误，也可以指情感不真诚。

usage_groups.group_title: 说法或表情中的虚假性
common_patterns: expose the falsity of an argument

examples.sentence: The documents exposed the falsity of his claim.
examples.translation: 这些文件揭露了他这一说法的虚假性。
examples.teaching_note: the falsity of a claim 表示某个说法不真实。

examples.sentence: The falsity of her smile made the apology seem insincere.
examples.translation: 她笑容中的虚情假意让这次道歉显得不真诚。
examples.teaching_note: 原书提到 smile，说明 falsity 也可指情感表达不真诚。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article repeated a dangerous falsehood.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: falsehood 常指一句具体的假话；falsity 更抽象。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness admitted that the statement was an untruth.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: untruth 比 falsity 更日常。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His insincerity was obvious during the apology.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: insincerity 强调情感或态度不真。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence revealed the truth of the matter.
antonyms.difference_note: truth 与 falsity 直接相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The rumor gave a false impression of the event.
The researcher was accused of falsifying data.
The falsification of records damaged the company’s reputation.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， (在文学或艺术作品中 ) 描述;描绘 depict (someone or something) in a work of art or literature: The novel portrays life in a small southern town. 小说描述了一个南方小镇的生活 。 v.t， 把 … 描述成 … describe (someone or something) in a particular way: He portrayed himself as a victim. 他把自己刻画成受害者 。 9 portrayal /po:'treral/ n. 描述; 描绘 the act or process or an instance of portraying; representation: a realistic portrayal of war 战争的真实描述

headline.core_en: to describe or represent someone or something in art, literature, or a particular way

familiar_bridge.summary: describe 是普通的“描述”；portray 更像通过小说、电影、绘画或评论把某人某事呈现成某种样子。

core.main: portray 既可以说文学艺术作品中的描绘，也可以说把某人塑造成某种形象。重点是呈现方式。

usage_groups.group_title: 在作品中描绘人物、生活或场景

examples.sentence: The novel portrays life in a small southern town.
examples.translation: 这部小说描绘了一个南方小镇的生活。
examples.teaching_note: 这是原书中的核心语境：文学作品中的描绘。

usage_groups.group_title: 把某人或某事描述成某种形象

examples.sentence: He portrayed himself as a victim of unfair criticism.
examples.translation: 他把自己描述成不公正批评的受害者。
examples.teaching_note: portray A as B 表示把 A 塑造成 B 的形象。

examples.sentence: The film portrays the scientist as both brilliant and deeply flawed.
examples.translation: 这部电影把这位科学家刻画成既才华出众又有深刻缺陷的人。
examples.teaching_note: portray 常用于评论人物形象是否复杂。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The guide describes each painting in clear, simple language.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: describe 更普通；portray 更强调呈现出的形象。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The painting depicts a crowded market.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: depict 与 portray 接近，常用于视觉或文学呈现。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel represents the city as a place of constant change.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: represent 更宽泛，可指表现、代表或象征。

antonyms.example_sentence: The article misrepresented the researcher’s findings.
antonyms.difference_note: misrepresent 是错误或歪曲地呈现。

word_family.example_sentence: The film offers a realistic portrayal of war.
The biography is a portrait of a restless mind.

headline.core_en: a person or thing that precipitates an event

familiar_bridge.summary: 在 catalyst 这类表达里，catalyst 的核心是“促进因素”。

examples.sentence: He was rumored to be the catalyst in a native uprising.
examples.translation: 这句原书/来源例句体现了“促进因素”。
examples.teaching_note: catalyst 在句中和具体对象搭配，语气比较自然。

examples.sentence: The article discussed catalyst as part of a broader argument.
examples.translation: 这里 catalyst 表示“促进因素”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new software sped up the search.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: speed up 更口语，表示让过程更快。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy accelerated economic change.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accelerate 更正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: A late decision delayed the project.
antonyms.difference_note: delay 与加速相反。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. SAE; 低估 reduce or underestimate the worth or importance of: J resent the way people seem to devalue my achievement. 人们似乎不重视我的成就 ， 这让我很反感 。 #3094) underrate

headline.core_en: to reduce or underestimate the value or importance of something

familiar_bridge.summary: value 是“价值”；devalue 就是让某物显得不值钱、不重要，或实际降低其价值。

core.main: devalue 既可指货币贬值，也可指低估某人的成就、劳动或贡献。GRE 阅读中常见的是抽象价值被贬低。

usage_groups.group_title: 降低某物的重要性或价值

examples.sentence: I resent the way people seem to devalue my achievement.
examples.translation: 人们似乎不重视我的成就，这让我很反感。
examples.teaching_note: 原书语境体现了 devalue 的“低估、不重视”。

examples.sentence: A narrow focus on profit can devalue forms of work that are socially important.
examples.translation: 只关注利润可能会贬低某些具有社会重要性的劳动。
examples.teaching_note: devalue 常用于抽象价值被压低的语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The market undervalued the company’s long-term potential.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: undervalue 和 devalue 接近，强调估价太低。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The currency depreciated after the crisis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: depreciate 常用于货币或资产价值下降。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay disparages popular culture as trivial.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disparage 更强调言语上轻视或贬低。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum helped visitors appreciate local art.
antonyms.difference_note: appreciate 可表示重视或价值上升，和 devalue 相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The community values public education.
Currency devaluation made imports more expensive.
Critics often undervalue the difficulty of the work.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 使从属于 …; 使地位位于 … 之下 to think of or treat (someone or something) as less important than someone or something else: subordinate one's personal interest to the public interest 使个人利益服从公众利益 | subordinate the passions to reason 使感情服从理智

headline.core_en: to treat something as less important than something else

familiar_bridge.summary: under 是“在下面”；subordinate 的感觉就是把一个东西放到另一个东西下面，让它服从更重要的目标。

core.main: subordinate 作动词时表示“使 A 服从于 B、把 A 放在 B 之下”。它也可作形容词或名词，表示“下级的；下属”。

usage_groups.group_title: 把较小目标放在更大目标之下
common_patterns: subordinate personal interest to public interest

examples.sentence: A responsible leader should subordinate personal interest to the public interest.
examples.translation: 负责任的领导者应使个人利益服从公共利益。

examples.sentence: The philosopher argued that passion should be subordinated to reason.
examples.translation: 这位哲学家认为感情应服从理智。
examples.teaching_note: 被动形式 be subordinated to 也很常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 组织或等级中处于下级

examples.sentence: The assistant played a subordinate role in the negotiations.
examples.translation: 这位助理在谈判中处于次要角色。
examples.teaching_note: 作形容词时，subordinate 表示地位或作用较低。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lower courts reviewed the case first.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lower 更普通；subordinate 更强调等级或服从关系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The financial issue was secondary to the ethical problem.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: secondary 强调重要性较低；subordinate 强调服从更高目标。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The institution became subservient to political pressure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: subservient 比 subordinate 更负面，常暗示失去独立性。

antonyms.example_sentence: The primary goal was to protect public health.
antonyms.difference_note: primary 与 subordinate 的次要地位相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The essay criticizes the subordination of science to politics.
The manager consulted her subordinates before making a decision.
The officer was punished for insubordinate behavior.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. ih; 预言 the practice of seeking knowledge of the future or the unknown by supernatural means: divination of the future 对将来的预仆 | The divination of the priest was fulfilled. 教士的预言应验了

headline.core_en: the attempt to discover the future or hidden knowledge by supernatural means

familiar_bridge.summary: predict 是“预测”；divination 更神秘，指通过占卜、神谕等超自然方式预知未知。

core.main: divination 不是普通预测，而是带有宗教、神秘或古代仪式色彩的“求知未来”。阅读中常用它区分理性推断和迷信式预言。

usage_groups.group_title: 通过神秘方式预知未来
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于祭司、神谕、古代仪式或迷信活动试图知道未来。

examples.sentence: The villagers relied on divination to decide when to plant their crops.
examples.translation: 村民依靠占卜来决定什么时候播种。
examples.teaching_note: practice/rely on divination 常表示把决定交给神秘预兆。

examples.sentence: The historian treats the ritual as a form of divination rather than as a scientific prediction.
examples.translation: 历史学家把这种仪式看作一种占卜，而不是科学预测。
examples.teaching_note: 这里突出了 divination 和 rational prediction 的区别。

usage_groups.group_title: 像预言一样判断未来走向
usage_groups.when_to_use: 也可在评论中带一点批评意味，指某种判断看起来像预言而非证据推理。

examples.sentence: Much of the essay sounds like political divination, offering confident claims about events that no one can foresee.
examples.translation: 这篇文章很多地方听起来像政治预言，对没人能预见的事件作出自信断言。
examples.teaching_note: 这种用法常带讽刺，暗示判断缺乏证据。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her prediction about the election proved wrong.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: prediction 可以是理性预测；divination 强调神秘或超自然方式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The prophecy promised the return of a lost king.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prophecy 常指神启式预言；divination 更强调获得预言的方法或实践。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The flight of birds was read as an augury of victory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: augury 更偏“预兆”的解读，常见于古典语境。

antonyms.example_sentence: Evidence-based prediction relies on data rather than omens.
antonyms.difference_note: 与 divination 的神秘方式形成对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Weather prediction uses observation and models.
confusable_words.difference_note: prediction 不一定神秘；divination 通常带超自然色彩。

word_family.example_sentence: In older usage, divine can mean to discover something by intuition or supernatural means.
The diviner interpreted the signs as a warning.

related_words.meaning: 神谕；传达神谕的人或地方
related_words.example_sentence: The oracle gave an answer that could be read in several ways.
The prophet warned that the city would fall.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 迷宫 a complicated irregular network of passages or paths in which it is difficult to find one's way; amaze: an immense underground labyrinth 巨大的地下迷宫 9 labyrinthine /lebo'rm@am/ adj. 迷宫般的; 极为复杂的 of, relating to, or resembling a labyrinth; intricate, involved: labyrinthine streets 迷官般的街道

headline.core_en: a maze-like network of passages or a very complicated situation

familiar_bridge.summary: maze 是日常的“迷宫”；labyrinth 更书面，既可指真正的迷宫，也可指让人难以理清的制度、街道或问题。

core.main: labyrinth 的重点是“进去以后很难找到路”。这个“路”可以是真实道路，也可以是复杂程序、权力结构或思想脉络。

usage_groups.group_title: 真实或空间上的迷宫
usage_groups.when_to_use: 描述地下通道、街巷、建筑结构等让人难以找到方向。

examples.sentence: Tourists often get lost in the labyrinth of narrow streets.
examples.translation: 游客常常在错综复杂的窄街中迷路。
examples.teaching_note: a labyrinth of streets 是非常自然的空间用法。

examples.sentence: The explorers entered an underground labyrinth of tunnels.
examples.translation: 探险者进入了一片地下隧道迷宫。
examples.teaching_note: 这里接近原书 an immense underground labyrinth 的语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 复杂到难以理清的制度或问题
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 bureaucracy, regulations, arguments, procedures 等抽象对象。

examples.sentence: Applicants must navigate a labyrinth of forms and regulations.
examples.translation: 申请者必须应付一套错综复杂的表格和规定。
examples.teaching_note: navigate a labyrinth 表示一步步穿过复杂系统。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The children found their way through the maze.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: maze 更日常；labyrinth 更书面，也更常用于抽象复杂系统。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The investigation uncovered a tangle of financial arrangements.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tangle 强调缠在一起；labyrinth 强调像迷宫一样难走出。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The complexity of the system discouraged reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: complexity 是抽象名词；labyrinth 更有“迷路”的画面。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new policy replaced the labyrinth with a straight path for applicants.
antonyms.difference_note: straight path 与 labyrinth 的绕和乱相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The old city was famous for its labyrinthine streets.

related_words.example_sentence: The museum built a maze for children.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. ir; 邻近 nearness in space, time, or relationship: be in close proximity to 非常接近于 | marriages in proximity of blood 近亲婚姻

headline.core_en: nearness in space, time, or relationship

familiar_bridge.summary: near 是简单的“近”；proximity 是名词，更正式，常说 in close proximity to，表示空间、时间或关系上接近。

core.main: proximity 不只是“距离近”，也可以指时间接近、关系接近或因靠近而产生影响。GRE 阅读中常用它解释原因、影响或分类。

usage_groups.group_title: 空间上距离很近
usage_groups.when_to_use: 描述建筑、地点、人群、物体之间的位置关系。
common_patterns: the proximity of A to B

examples.sentence: The school is in close proximity to several museums.
examples.translation: 这所学校离几家博物馆很近。
examples.teaching_note: in close proximity to 是最常见结构。

examples.sentence: The proximity of the factory to the river raised environmental concerns.
examples.translation: 工厂离河太近，引发了环境方面的担忧。
examples.teaching_note: the proximity of A to B 用于说明靠近带来的影响。

usage_groups.group_title: 时间或关系上的接近
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于事件发生时间接近，或人群、血缘、概念之间关系接近。

examples.sentence: The temporal proximity of the two events made a causal link seem plausible.
examples.translation: 两个事件在时间上非常接近，使因果联系看起来有可能。
examples.teaching_note: temporal proximity 是阅读中讨论因果时常见的表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The nearness of the storm worried the sailors.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: nearness 更普通；proximity 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The adjacency of the two buildings made expansion difficult.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: adjacency 更强调紧挨着；proximity 可以只是较近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Their closeness made cooperation easier.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: closeness 可指情感亲密；proximity 更中性、正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: Distance from the capital limited the town’s influence.
antonyms.difference_note: distance 与 proximity 的接近相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The proximate cause of the fire was faulty wiring.
The trip takes approximately two hours.

related_words.example_sentence: The numbers are approximate, not exact.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 'ULM; 41474289 promising or suggesting good fortune or happiness; hopeful: rosy prospects 美好的前景 | arosy view 乐观的看法

headline.core_cn: 乐观的；前景美好的；红润的
headline.core_en: hopeful and promising, or pink like a rose

familiar_bridge.summary: rose 是“玫瑰”；rosy 原本有玫瑰般红润的画面，后来常用来形容前景、看法很乐观。

core.main: rosy 在 GRE 语境中常说 rosy prospects / a rosy view，重点是“把未来看得很好”。它可以是真乐观，也可能暗示过于乐观。

usage_groups.group_title: 前景或看法显得乐观

examples.sentence: Investors had a rosy view of the company’s future.
examples.translation: 投资者对这家公司未来抱有乐观的看法。
examples.teaching_note: 这里用 rosy 修饰 view，表示对未来判断偏乐观。

examples.sentence: The report paints a rosy picture of the economy, but unemployment remains high.
examples.translation: 报告把经济描绘得很乐观，但失业率仍然很高。
examples.teaching_note: paint a rosy picture 常暗示这种乐观可能不完全可靠。

usage_groups.group_title: 脸色红润或颜色玫瑰色

examples.sentence: After the walk in the cold air, the child had rosy cheeks.
examples.translation: 在冷空气中散步后，孩子脸颊红润。
examples.teaching_note: 这是 rosy 最直观的颜色义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The early results made the researchers hopeful.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hopeful 直接表示有希望；rosy 更常修饰前景或看法。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The forecast was optimistic about future growth.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: optimistic 常修饰人或预测；rosy 更有“前景美好”的色彩。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new treatment produced promising results.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: promising 强调有成功可能；rosy 更强调看法乐观。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company faced a bleak future after the scandal.
antonyms.difference_note: bleak 与 rosy prospects 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Analysts offered a gloomy forecast for the industry.
antonyms.difference_note: gloomy forecast 与 rosy forecast 对照明显。

word_family.example_sentence: The color was a deep rose.
He saw the past through rose-colored memories.

related_words.example_sentence: Her optimism helped the team continue.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. #2; 复杂; 高级 the process or result of becoming more complex, developed, or subtle: the technological sophistication of their products 他们产品技术上的高级 n. 有智慧; 见多识广 the process or result of becoming cultured, knowledgeable, or disillusioned; especially cultivation, urbanity: her air of sophistication 她博学的气质

headline.core_cn: 复杂精密；老练；见多识广
headline.core_en: the quality of being complex and refined, or cultured and worldly

familiar_bridge.summary: simple 的反面不一定只是 difficult；sophistication 更像“变得复杂、精细、有层次”，也可指人见多识广、很老练。

core.main: sophistication 有两条常见线：技术、产品、理论更复杂精密；人或风格更成熟、老练、有文化修养。不要把它简单理解成“难”。

usage_groups.group_title: 技术、产品或理论复杂精密
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 technology, products, systems, analysis 等，强调发展程度高、结构精细。

examples.sentence: The technological sophistication of the device made it difficult for competitors to copy.
examples.translation: 这款设备的技术复杂度很高，使竞争者难以复制。
examples.teaching_note: technological sophistication 来自原书语境，强调技术高级而精密。

examples.sentence: The model lacks the sophistication needed to explain real human behavior.
examples.translation: 这个模型缺少解释真实人类行为所需的复杂精细程度。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用 sophistication 评价理论或模型是否足够精密。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或风格显得老练有修养

examples.sentence: Her calm manner gave her an air of sophistication.
examples.translation: 她沉着的举止让她显得很老练、有修养。
examples.teaching_note: an air of sophistication 是原书语境里的自然表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The complexity of the machine increased its cost.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: complexity 只说复杂；sophistication 常带“高级、精细”的正面意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The design shows great refinement in every detail.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: refinement 更强调精致和打磨；sophistication 可指复杂精密或世故老练。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His worldliness made him hard to impress.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: worldliness 偏人情世故；sophistication 更中性，可褒可贬。

antonyms.example_sentence: The simplicity of the design made it easy to use.
antonyms.difference_note: simplicity 与技术或结构上的 sophistication 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her naivety made the deception easy.
antonyms.difference_note: naivety 与人的老练相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The company sells sophisticated medical equipment.
The novel appealed to urban sophisticates.

related_words.example_sentence: The argument sounded clever, but it was mere sophistry.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 壮观的; 壮丽的 causing wonder and admiration; very impressive: a spectacular display of fireworks on New Year's Eve 除夕燃放的壮观的烟火近兴词 outstanding

headline.core_en: very impressive and exciting to see or notice

familiar_bridge.summary: spectacle 是“场面、景象”；spectacular 就是像大型场面一样让人惊叹、印象强烈。

core.main: spectacular 不只是 good，而是“视觉上或效果上非常惊人”。它常修饰 display, view, success, failure, rise, collapse 等。

usage_groups.group_title: 景象或表演非常壮观

examples.sentence: The city put on a spectacular display of fireworks on New Year’s Eve.
examples.translation: 除夕夜，这座城市燃放了一场壮观的烟火。
examples.teaching_note: a spectacular display of fireworks 接近原书例句。

examples.sentence: From the mountain, we had a spectacular view of the valley.
examples.translation: 从山上看去，我们能看到山谷的壮丽景色。
examples.teaching_note: spectacular view 是最自然的视觉搭配。

usage_groups.group_title: 结果、成功或失败非常惊人

examples.sentence: The policy produced spectacular growth in its first year.
examples.translation: 这项政策在第一年带来了惊人的增长。
examples.teaching_note: 这里不是视觉壮观，而是结果非常显著。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The performance was impressive.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: impressive 更宽泛；spectacular 更强，常带壮观或惊人效果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The painting made a striking impression on visitors.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: striking 强调马上抓住注意力；spectacular 更强调规模和效果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The palace was magnificent in the morning light.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: magnificent 更庄严华丽；spectacular 更偏“场面惊人”。

antonyms.example_sentence: The result was ordinary, not spectacular.
antonyms.difference_note: ordinary 与 spectacular 的强烈效果相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The parade was a spectacle of color and sound.
Spectators filled the stadium.

related_words.example_sentence: The plan failed spectacularly.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 永生; 不朽 the ability to live forever; eternal life: He believes in the immortality of the soul. 他相信灵魂不灭 。 | the immortality of the great achievements of Newton and Einstein 牛顿和爱 SSH Fe Mb BY AG

headline.core_en: the state of living forever or being remembered forever

familiar_bridge.summary: mortal 是“会死的”；immortal 是“不死的、不朽的”；immortality 就是“永生”或“名声、成就不朽”。

core.main: immortality 有两层常见意思：灵魂或生命不死；成就、作品或名声长久流传。GRE 阅读中常用于宗教、哲学和文化评价。

usage_groups.group_title: 生命或灵魂不死
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 soul, gods, afterlife 等宗教或哲学语境。

examples.sentence: He believes in the immortality of the soul.
examples.translation: 他相信灵魂不灭。
examples.teaching_note: the immortality of the soul 是原书中的核心搭配。

examples.sentence: Many myths describe heroes who search for immortality.
examples.translation: 许多神话描写英雄寻找永生。
examples.teaching_note: search/seek immortality 常见于神话和哲学语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 成就或名声长久流传
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 achievements, works, fame, legacy 等文化评价。

examples.sentence: The discoveries of Newton and Einstein gave them a kind of scientific immortality.
examples.translation: 牛顿和爱因斯坦的发现让他们获得了某种科学上的不朽。
examples.teaching_note: 这里不是肉体永生，而是成就被长期记住。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The religion promises eternal life to the faithful.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: eternal life 更直接指死后或宗教意义上的永生。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The trust was designed to preserve the land in perpetuity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: perpetuity 更法律或抽象，不一定指生命。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The composer achieved enduring fame after his death.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: enduring fame 对应 immortality 的“不朽声名”义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The poem reflects on human mortality.
antonyms.difference_note: mortality 是 mortal 的名词，与 immortality 直接相反。

word_family.example_sentence: All mortal beings eventually die.
The poet hoped his work would be immortal.
The statue immortalized the city’s founder.

related_words.example_sentence: Medical advances reduced infant mortality.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: ad. 不重要的; 没有关系的; 没有影响的 not connected with or relevant to something: irrelevant remarks 无关紧要的言论 i S44) extraneous, impertinent, immaterial, insignificant

headline.core_en: not connected with or useful to the matter being discussed

familiar_bridge.summary: relevant 是“相关的”；irrelevant 加上否定前缀 ir-，就是“和当前问题没有关系”。

core.main: irrelevant 的重点不是“完全不重要”，而是“对眼前这个问题、论证或决定没有帮助”。阅读中常用它排除干扰信息。

usage_groups.group_title: 信息或评论与当前问题无关

examples.sentence: The judge ruled that the witness’s private life was irrelevant to the case.
examples.translation: 法官裁定证人的私生活与本案无关。

examples.sentence: The paragraph contains several vivid but irrelevant details.
examples.translation: 这段文字有几个生动但无关的细节。
examples.teaching_note: irrelevant details 常用于批评写作或论证跑题。

usage_groups.group_title: 因素对判断或结果没有影响
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 fact, issue, factor, difference 等判断条件。

examples.sentence: For the purposes of this study, the artist’s personal beliefs are largely irrelevant.
examples.translation: 就这项研究而言，艺术家的个人信念基本无关。
examples.teaching_note: largely irrelevant 表示某因素不该影响当前分析。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The two events were unrelated.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unrelated 更普通；irrelevant 强调对当前问题没有用。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor removed extraneous material from the essay.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: extraneous 常指多余、外加的内容。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness’s age was immaterial to the legal question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: immaterial 常用于法律或正式判断中。

antonyms.example_sentence: Only relevant evidence should be considered.
antonyms.difference_note: relevant 与 irrelevant 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She asked a pertinent question about the data.
antonyms.difference_note: pertinent 比 relevant 更强调切中当前讨论。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The error was insignificant and did not affect the result.
confusable_words.difference_note: insignificant 说影响小；irrelevant 说不相关。

word_family.example_sentence: The article provides relevant background.
The relevance of the evidence was disputed.

related_words.example_sentence: The irrelevance of the comment annoyed the committee.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 732; 包含; 涉及 involve (something) in a necessary way: Local diseases often implicate a general derangement of the system. 局部疾病会扰乱整个系统

headline.core_cn: 牵涉；暗示有责任；必然包含
headline.core_en: to show that someone or something is involved, or that something necessarily follows

familiar_bridge.summary: involve 是“涉及”；implicate 更强，常表示“把某人牵连进责任或问题里”，也可指一个事实必然包含另一个结果。

core.main: implicate 有两条线：证据把某人牵涉进坏事；一个现象或理论暗含、涉及更大的问题。GRE 阅读中常见第二种，更像“牵出一个含义”。

usage_groups.group_title: 证据把某人牵涉进问题
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 evidence, witness, investigation 等法律或责任语境。

examples.sentence: The new evidence implicated several officials in the scandal.
examples.translation: 新的证据把几名官员牵涉进这桩丑闻。
examples.teaching_note: implicate someone in something 是最常见责任语境。

examples.sentence: No witness was able to implicate him directly.
examples.translation: 没有证人能够直接证明他涉案。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 implicate 带“牵涉责任”的意味。

usage_groups.group_title: 一个事实牵出更大的含义
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 theory, disease, finding, argument 等，说明某事和更大系统或后果相连。

examples.sentence: Local diseases often implicate a general disorder of the system.
examples.translation: 局部疾病常常涉及整个系统的失调。
examples.teaching_note: 接近原书例句，implicate 表示局部问题牵出整体问题。

examples.sentence: The discovery implicates climate change in the decline of the species.
examples.translation: 这一发现表明气候变化与该物种减少有关。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用 implicate 表示“把某因素纳入解释”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The project involves several departments.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: involve 中性；implicate 常带责任、问题或必然后果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The documents incriminated the company’s directors.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incriminate 更强，直接指罪责。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform would entail major costs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: entail 强调逻辑或实际上的必然后果。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new evidence exonerated the suspect.
antonyms.difference_note: exonerate 与 implicate someone in wrongdoing 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: His silence seemed to imply agreement.
confusable_words.difference_note: imply 只说暗示；implicate 常涉及责任或复杂后果。

word_family.example_sentence: The policy has serious implications for schools.
The argument rests on an implicit assumption.

related_words.example_sentence: New evidence complicated the investigation.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: #744 n. 意义; 影响; 潜在的后果 a likely consequence of something: The book has political implications. 这本书有政治影响

headline.core_en: a possible meaning, consequence, or effect of something

familiar_bridge.summary: meaning 是普通的“意思”；implication 更像“这件事背后会带出什么含义或后果”。

core.main: implication 不只是字面意思，而是一个事实、观点、政策可能带来的“潜在后果”或“更深层含义”。阅读中常用来讨论研究发现、政策和论证的影响。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点或事实背后的含义
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 statement, argument, discovery, finding 等，表示没有明说但可推出的意思。

examples.sentence: The implication of her argument is that tradition alone cannot justify the policy.
examples.translation: 她的论证含义是：仅凭传统不能证明这项政策合理。
examples.teaching_note: the implication of an argument 表示从论证中推出的深层意思。

examples.sentence: He did not accuse them directly, but the implication was obvious.
examples.translation: 他没有直接指责他们，但言外之意很明显。
examples.teaching_note: the implication was obvious 是常见表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 政策、发现或选择带来的后果

examples.sentence: The book has political implications that extend beyond its literary value.
examples.translation: 这本书具有超出文学价值的政治影响。
examples.teaching_note: political implications 接近原书例句。

examples.sentence: The study’s implications for public health are still being debated.
examples.translation: 这项研究对公共卫生的影响仍在讨论中。
examples.teaching_note: implications for 是学术阅读中的高频结构。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The meaning of the phrase is unclear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: meaning 更宽泛；implication 强调间接含义或后果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision had serious consequences.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: consequence 更直接指结果；implication 可指潜在影响。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform had ramifications for the entire school system.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ramification 比 implication 更强调复杂分支后果。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author makes an explicit statement of her position.
antonyms.difference_note: explicit statement 是直接说出；implication 是间接推出。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The inference is reasonable, but not certain.
confusable_words.difference_note: inference 是读者或研究者推出的结论；implication 是材料本身带出的含义。

word_family.example_sentence: The evidence implies a different conclusion.
The contract contains an implicit promise.
The findings implicate several environmental factors.

related_words.example_sentence: The instructions were explicit and easy to follow.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 享有特权的; 地位高的 having special rights, advantages, or immunities: get privileged treatment 受到优待 | one's privileged status 某人的特权地位

headline.core_en: having special rights, advantages, or social status

familiar_bridge.summary: privilege 是“特权”；privileged 形容人或群体时，表示他们拥有别人没有的资源、机会或保护。

core.main: privileged 不只是“有钱”，而是处在制度或社会结构中更有利的位置。阅读中常用它讨论阶层、教育、法律保护或待遇不平等。

usage_groups.group_title: 享有特殊机会或待遇
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 享有特权的；受到优待的

examples.sentence: Children from privileged families often have access to better schools.
examples.translation: 来自优越家庭的孩子往往能进入更好的学校。
examples.teaching_note: privileged families 强调社会资源和机会上的优势。

examples.sentence: The law gave certain companies privileged treatment.
examples.translation: 这项法律给予某些公司优惠待遇。
examples.teaching_note: privileged treatment 接近原书搭配，指制度性优待。

usage_groups.group_title: 法律或职业上受保护的信息

examples.sentence: The lawyer refused to reveal privileged communication with her client.
examples.translation: 律师拒绝透露与客户之间受特权保护的通信。
examples.teaching_note: 这是正式法律语境中的用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The favored group received extra support.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: favored 更普通；privileged 更常指制度或地位带来的优势。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Advantaged students often enter school with more preparation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: advantaged 直接指资源优势；privileged 更强调特权或身份。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Elite institutions often serve a narrow segment of society.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: elite 强调高层或精英；privileged 强调享有特殊优势。

antonyms.example_sentence: The program supports disadvantaged communities.
antonyms.difference_note: disadvantaged 与 privileged 的社会位置相反。

word_family.example_sentence: Access to clean water should be a right, not a privilege.

word_family.meaning: 没有特权的；处境不利的
word_family.example_sentence: Unprivileged children often lack access to early education.

related_words.example_sentence: Early training gave her an advantage.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 共情 (能理解或感受到他人的感受 ) the ability to understand and share the feelings of another: He felt great empathy with the poor. 他很能感受到穷人的处境

headline.core_en: the ability to understand and share another person’s feelings

familiar_bridge.summary: sympathy 是“同情”；empathy 更强调站到对方感受里，理解他为什么会痛苦、害怕或高兴。

core.main: empathy 的重点是“感受到并理解他人的处境”。它不一定等于赞同对方，而是能理解对方的感受。

usage_groups.group_title: 理解他人的感受和处境
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 people, patients, readers, leaders, teachers 等需要理解他人感受的场景。

examples.sentence: He felt great empathy with the poor.
examples.translation: 他很能体会穷人的处境。
examples.teaching_note: feel empathy with someone 接近原书例句。

examples.sentence: A good doctor needs empathy as well as technical skill.
examples.translation: 一位好医生既需要专业技术，也需要同理心。
examples.teaching_note: empathy 常用于医疗、教育、领导等人际语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 文学或艺术让人进入他人经验
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 literature, art, narrative 等，说明作品帮助读者理解他人经验。

examples.sentence: The novel creates empathy for characters who are usually ignored.
examples.translation: 这部小说让读者对通常被忽视的人物产生共情。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读评论中常用 empathy 说明作品的情感效果。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her understanding made the conversation easier.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: understanding 较宽泛；empathy 特别指对感受的理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Compassion led the volunteers to help the refugees.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: compassion 带行动和关怀；empathy 可以只是感受和理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She expressed sympathy for the victims.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sympathy 更像旁观者同情；empathy 更像进入对方感受。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official’s indifference angered the public.
antonyms.difference_note: indifference 是缺乏关心，与 empathy 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: His sympathy was sincere, but he did not fully understand her fear.
confusable_words.difference_note: sympathy 不一定真正理解对方感受。

word_family.example_sentence: An empathetic teacher notices when students are struggling.
Readers can empathize with the narrator’s loneliness.

related_words.example_sentence: The scene has pathos without becoming sentimental.
